summary: local-mcp posts host and environment data to attacker-controlled Railway endpoint at install time
details: The package ships three install/runtime scripts (setup.js, index.js, download.js) that import child_process, os, fs, http, and https together and contact a hardcoded endpoint at https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app — a free Railway-hosted host that does not match any documented publisher infrastructure for a package named 'local-mcp'. setup.js performs multiple POST requests to remote endpoints, while index.js issues https.get/http.get calls to the same Railway host alongside reads of process.env and process.platform, exhibiting the host-fingerprint + environment-data + outbound-network shape characteristic of installer-side exfiltration. download.js additionally fetches from this Railway host and from local-mcp.com / download.local-mcp.com to stage further content. The combination of (a) a hardcoded non-publisher C2-style endpoint on a free PaaS, (b) process.env reads next to the network call, (c) child_process available in the same script, and (d) staged content download from auxiliary domains is consistent with an active credential/host-info exfiltration and second-stage dropper. Installers running 'npm install local-mcp' or loading the module would have host metadata and environment variables transmitted to the operator of the Railway app and would execute attacker-supplied content fetched from the download endpoints.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
